title: Make the Formula value available in API and integration (like Make)
excerpt:
If the formula value was available via API or integration that would be easier. Or create an automation that can easily put the result of a formula in a regular number column. 
When I created a summary colum of my subite…

posts.author: frossini (Fab Rossini)
posts.cookedHtml:
If the formula value was available via API or integration that would be easier. Or create an automation that can easily put the result of a formula in a regular number column.
When I created a summary colum of my subitems on my item line, Monday created a formula column to make the sum, but I cannot use this variable with Make, automation or any API…

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @frossini

The outcome of the formula column is calculated at client side (your browser) and with this implementation it is not possible to access the outcome anywhere (automations, integrations, Make, etc).

For this specific issue (show summary of subitems in parent) there is an alternative approach with the app Rollup Subitems. This app does summarize subitem columns to the parent item and put the outcome in a normal column that can be used elsewhere. It also supports filtering in case you only want to summarize a subset of the subitems.

posts.author: frossini (Fab Rossini)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi, Thank you for your answer. I understand for the API.

It would be great if what the app does (for a price) could be done in Monday natively. Or at least add the possibility to transfer the data from a formula column to another regular one so we can use it in automations without the need of another app.

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @frossini,

Thank you for that feedback!

I will make sure to add your vote for this request

Cheers,
Matias

posts.author: Chester (Chester Alvin Escorpiso)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Matias,

I’ve seen some similar posts about formula fields and now I understand that the outcome of the formula lives locally and not on monday.com’s side. But how about the formula itself? Is there a way we can lookup the formula (not the outcome), this way we can just map it on the other platform.

I use zapier a lot and if I can get the (fx) that the formula field uses, it will be so much easier to set that value somewhere else.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
The formula itself is available through the API. Try this:

{boards(ids:123456789){columns (types: formula) {title settings_str}}}

and see:

{
  "data": {
    "boards": [
      {
        "columns": [
          {
            "title": "Formula",
            "type": "formula",
            "settings_str": "{\"formula\":**\"{numbers__1}/5\"**}"
          }
        ]
      }
    ]
  },
  "account_id": 123456
}```

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you @basdebruin for the help here!

posts.author: DanielHai (Daniel Hai)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Everyone

I am super excited to share the formula column value is now available to read using the Platform API!

monday.com Platform API

New ability to read the formula column

In API versions 2025-01 and later, you can read the formula column using the display_value field. This field returns the content of the formula column as a string. This feature is in an early release phase and has the following limitations: Formulas...

This open the doors to a lot of new possibilities, such as creating brand new types of apps or extending existing ones to now also support the formula column.

If you need its result also available thorugh 3rd party tools like make.com, I reccomend contacting them directly to add support.

Looking forward to your feedback.

Happy Coding!

posts.author: chadhutchins10 (Chad Hutchins)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is exactly what we’ve been needing, but I’m unable to get this to work. Has anyone else been able to get it to work?

I’ve set my API-Version header to 2025-01 but I always get the following error when trying to use display_value:

Field 'display_value' doesn't exist on type 'FormulaValue'

Here is an example query:

{
  items(ids: 5390625456) {
    id
    name
    column_values {
      id
      value
      text
      type
      ... on FormulaValue {
        display_value
      }
    }
  }
}

Any ideas?

posts.author: DanielHai (Daniel Hai)
posts.cookedHtml:
Looks like its still using the older version.

My recommendation

  1. First try making the query from the API playground in the developer center, be sure to change the version to 2025-01
  2. You can add the version field to your query so that you will know what version was actually used to resolve the query.
    version{
    display_name
    }

posts.author: chadhutchins10 (Chad Hutchins)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thanks Daniel, I tried a few things but still no luck.

I can’t seem to find the “version selector” in the playground. Did that get removed? However by setting the header and using the version { value }, the playground does show it’s using 2025-01.

When using our normal API calls in our code, I still get the same error. We send the API-Version: 2025-01 header in our calls and Monday shows the api-version: 2025-01 in the response.

Is this only enabled on certain accounts?

I’m using the exact query provided on the change log note (with the exception of changing the board ID).

Anything else I can try?

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello there @chadhutchins10,

That is odd. It should work for you if the API version 2025-01 is being added.

Would you be able to please fill this form adding as much information as possible to it so that our team can take a look into it?

posts.author: chadhutchins10 (Chad Hutchins)
posts.cookedHtml: Thank you @Matias.Monday I’ve just now submitted the ticket #3759563 thanks!

posts.author: rgrv13 (REMI)
posts.cookedHtml: same issue for me, did you get an answer ? very useful feature, unfortunately doest work …

posts.author: chadhutchins10 (Chad Hutchins)
posts.cookedHtml:
@rgrv13 no solution yet, but I have new information.

  1. Version 2025-01 is only working on the API Playground, so anyone trying to use this in your custom code or api apps, it is not working as of December 10. It’s been escalated to the development team.

  2. That’s all fine and dandy EXCEPT the ability to change the API Version on the API Playground is not available for everyone. So there’s no real way to switch to the 2025-01 version and test it at the moment.

I’m hoping for a solution soon. Looking forward to trying this out. I’ll update this as I hear more.

posts.author: rgrv13 (REMI)
posts.cookedHtml: ahah funny That’s frustrating, the feature is so useful …

posts.author: idna (Andy)
posts.cookedHtml:
I also wanted to try this out.
In Postman the display_value is recognized by intellisense but the response is null when executed.

query {
  items (ids:[1234567890]) {
    column_values {
      ... on FormulaValue {
        value
        id
        display_value
      }
    }
  }
}

Response

{
    "data": {
        "boards": null
    },
    "account_id": 1234
}

posts.author: chadhutchins10 (Chad Hutchins)
posts.cookedHtml:
Monday support did let me know how to test this with the API Playground. There are actually 2 API Playgounds.

There is the public one on the documentation website: monday.com - you cannot change the version on this one

And there is one in your Developer Center: https://[your-subdomain].monday.com/apps/playground - this one allows you to change the API version. The formula display value IS working on this one.

Learn something new everyday!

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
@idna hi!

Are you sure you are passing a correct board ID that is accesible by the user whose API token you are using?

If so, please please fill this form adding as much information as possible to it (such as account ID, board IDs, item IDs, timestamps, etc.) so that our team can take a look into it!

posts.author: hellotimi (David Kalu (Formula Pro - Use formula column results in automations!))
posts.cookedHtml:
The Formula PRO app on the monday.com marketplace integrates seamlessly with the new Formula in Automations API, taking your native formula columns to the next level. This means you can now leverage powerful automations while working with the native formula functions without needing to learn new syntax.

With Formula PRO, you can project and write the results from your native formula columns to other columns (including those not supported by the native formula column), as well as to mirror columns and subitems. The integration with the API makes it easier than ever to automate and enhance your workflows.

Plus, Formula PRO includes 40 automation recipes, with new ones added every two weeks, allowing you to automate more tasks and streamline your processes.

Feel free to check it out here: Formula PRO : Advanced Automations for Native Formula Columns, Mirror Columns & Subitems!

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml: exclude_reply_from_sla

posts.author: sl1nna (Sami Linnanvuo)
posts.cookedHtml:
@Matias.Monday We are adding support for formula column in our app (Analytics & Reports by Screenful) and are facing some issues. When we try to import all items along with their formula column values we get an error message:

Monday API: Too many formula columns have been requested. Please reduce the number of columns.

This happens with an account that has a fairly small amount of items (<1000). Is this expected behavior? What kind of rate limits there are when reading formula values from the API? Are the limits based on the number of items requested, or the number of columns requested? Are there any workarounds?

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello there @sl1nna,

There is a maximum of 5 formula columns per request. It seems like the board might have more than 5 formula columns.

posts.author: sl1nna (Sami Linnanvuo)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for the clarification

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml: Happy to help @sl1nna !
